feed.title: ASmap dashboard: tracking the asmap-data history against the observed network

entry.title: ASmap dashboard: tracking the asmap-data history against the observed network
entry.summary: The evaluation of movements within the Bitcoin Network can be significantly enhanced by focusing on changes associated with IP addresses that host nodes. The relevance of a particular change or movement in the network is primarily determined by its connection to these nodes. Specifically, if a movement involves IP addresses that do not support any nodes, it tends to be less noteworthy. Conversely, changes that involve IP addresses hosting a substantial number of nodes warrant more attention and are considered more significant. This approach helps in prioritizing the examination of alterations within the network, ensuring that the focus is on areas with potential substantial impacts on the network’s functionality and security.
